channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Canceled Last of Us Online Called “Best Multiplayer Game” by Naughty Dog Devs
item.content:encoded:
The ex-director of the canceled The Last of Us Online game has revealed that former Naughty Dog colleagues still consider it the best multiplayer game.

The Last of Us online game was officially canceled in 2023. The ex-director recently revealed that he was informed of the cancellation just one day before the public announcement. Now, he has also shared that the response from his former colleagues at Naughty Dog regarding the game was very positive.

Canceled Last of Us Online’s Director Will Not Let Any Other Project Suffer the Same Fate

Spotted by GamesRadar+, the ex-director of the canceled The Last of Us Online title, Vinit Agarwal, recently took to X (formerly Twitter) to discuss the messages he received about the game to this day. Agarwal stated that ex-colleagues are still messaging him, talking about “how amazing TLOU Online was going to be — still the best multiplayer game they’ve ever played.” Agarwal added that he will never let any of his work “not see the light of day again.”

The developers announced in 2023 that, upon bringing the title into full production, they realized how massive the scope of this project was and had to stop development. Naughty Dog was presented with two choices: either become a studio that focuses solely on live-service games or “continue to focus on single-player narrative games that have defined Naughty Dog’s heritage,” and they chose the latter.

In other news, The Last of Us universe reportedly features a colony of immune people apart from Ellie. Additionally, the Elden Ring movie has secured an actor from HBO’s The Last of Us series. What are your thoughts on Naughty Dog devs calling the canceled Last of Us online title the best multiplayer game they have ever played? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Valve revealing why the Steam Controller launched before the Steam Machine. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: UFC 6 Release Date & All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Out of the blue, UFC 6 appeared, and it’s finally time for us all to share the octagon again. The real-life MMA sport continues to be one of the world’s most gripping, box-office combat spectacles, and EA is gearing up for the sixth mainline game.

With Alex Pereira and Max Holloway gracing the main UFC 6 covers, you’ll have a headache (thankfully, not through fighting) about which edition to pick up and who you want on the cover, but you need to know when it’s time for your ringwalk, and where you can actually fight!

When Does UFC 6 Come Out? UFC 6 Release Date, Explained

EA has given UFC 6 a June 19, 2026, release date.

UFC 6 hasn’t been in official existence for that long, but EA isn’t making us wait an eternity for the game. In fact, it’s a two-month window between the game’s unveiling and the launch date.

  • More Insider Gaming: All UFC 6 Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices, Explained

Pre-orders are open for UFC 6, and as with many EA sports titles, you can get early access and choose different editions, depending on how much content you want from the game.

Can You Play UFC 6 on PlayStation? PS5 Status, Explained

UFC 6 will be a day-one release on the PlayStation 5, but there won’t be a past-gen PS4 release.

One of UFC 6’s main platforms at launch is the PS5—which is the case for almost every non-Nintendo Switch game in 2026! Another thing we’ve come to expect in the modern day is new games lacking a last-gen port. UFC 5 also missed out on the PS4, and 2020’s UFC 4 will likely be the last UFC game to get this treatment.

Can You Play UFC 6 on Xbox? Xbox Series X|S Status, Explained

You will also be able to purchase and play UFC 6 on Xbox Series X|S devices.

Once again, Xbox gets a current-gen port, but there’s no last-gen edition. If you’ve got an Xbox One and haven’t upgraded to either of Microsoft’s newer hardware, then you’ll have to settle for UFC 4 for now.

Can You Play UFC 6 on PC?

A PC version is reportedly happening for UFC 6, but right now, no port has been announced, meaning technically, UFC 6 won’t be available to play on PC at launch.

According to our own Mike Straw, in the ‘EA Sports UFC 6 officially releases June 19, getting new game mode‘ story: ‘A PC version of the game is in development.’

However, that’s the extent of our knowledge so far. It’s likely a PC port will happen, but there’s no timeframe for now.

Can You Play UFC 6 on Nintendo Switch? Switch 2 Status, Explained

Nintendo owners have to miss out on UFC 6 altogether, with no Nintendo Switch or Switch 2 port planned.

If you are a Nintendo Switch owner desperate to enter the ring, you might want to throw down with EA to get your point across, as UFC 6 has no Switch plans.

It’s been three years since UFC 5, so what are you hoping that UFC 6 does differently this time around? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Read about former Prince of Persia Ubisoft devs hiring for a new game, and Resident Evil Requiem devs giving a first update on the game’s Story DLC for more content.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Former Prince of Persia Ubisoft Devs Hiring For New Game
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft Montpellier is looking for a ‘Technical Director Level Design’ for an ‘unannounced project.’ As you can imagine, this has already sent people into a frenzy speculating what the new game could be, and if Prince of Persia is about to be revitalized in some way.

You could argue Ubisoft is riding a massive tidal wave-sized high right now following the recent unveiling of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced. The legendary action game looks fantastic, and Ubisoft Singapore looks to be doing a great job, but after doing some digging, another Ubisoft studio is making some moves.

Ubisoft Montpellier Hiring For Unannounced Project

An Unannounced project is being advertised by Ubisoft Montpellier, with the official jobs page recruiting for a Technical Director Level Design, and the game is currently ‘still in the early concept phase.’

It requires extensive knowledge and understanding of Unreal Engine 5, and based on the job description, it’s thought that the title could be a fully fledged 3D game.

Now, it’s time for some deduction.

Ubisoft Montpellier is renowned for the Rayman franchise, and Insider Gaming has already confirmed that a Rayman Remake is happening, with future updates stating Ubisoft is working on ‘The Future of Rayman.’

An Insider Gaming exclusive report also revealed that Beyond Good & Evil 2 survived Ubisoft’s Project Purge in January 2026.

Curiously, part of this purge included the devastating loss of the Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time Remake. The game was moved from Ubisoft Mumbai and Ubisoft Pune to Ubisoft Montréal, but not even the Montréal studio could save the game.

Not only this, but Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown was developed by Ubisoft Montpellier. Despite its excellence, the studio behind it was later disbanded, leaving a Prince of Persia-sized gap in the market.

TheGameVerse claims:

‘This points to a 3D project (not a 2D sidescroller). With the Lost Crown team recently regrouped and pitching ideas, and PoP being the only major IP fitting Montpellier’s Creative House 4, this could be the next Prince of Persia!’

We’re not going to get anyone’s hopes up (including our own), as we haven’t heard anything on this, but it will be interesting to see what this unannounced project from Ubisoft Montpellier is.

Could the Prince be getting another shot? Or is it something else entirely? Get your guesses in through our Insider Gaming Discord.

The news doesn’t stop here. Read Valve revealing why the new Steam Controller is launching before the Steam Machine and Resident Evil Requiem devs giving the first update on story DLC.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

item.title: Beachcombing Map Condition In ARC Raiders—Where To Find Dockmaster’s Detector
item.content:encoded:
Map Conditions are a key part of what keeps ARC Raiders feeling fresh and exciting, as they can fundamentally change how a map is played. Not all of them are that drastic, though, and Riven Tides’ Beachcombing is a simple but notable Minor Map Condition.

Riven Tides is the biggest update ARC Raiders has seen yet, with a huge selection of content dropped at once. Whilst the new map and ARC threat are bound to steal the show, don’t sleep on Beachcombing, as there are some great rewards up for grabs.

If you’re wondering what’s different with Beachcombing, and you’re lost looking for the Dockmaster’s Detector, you’re in the right place; we have all the details below.

What Maps Is ‘Beachcombing’ On In ARC Raiders?

Beachcombing, the new Minor Map Condition in ARC Raiders, is exclusive to the new Riven Tides map.

Every hour, Map Conditions refresh in ARC Raiders, with Major and Minor Conditions shifting between the line-up. If you’re looking to jump into Beachcombing, though, you’ll need to keep your eye on Riven Tides, as it won’t feature anywhere else.

  • More Insider Gaming: ARC Raiders Event Tracker—Every Map Condition Schedule

It makes sense, too, since you’ll be scouring the vast, open beaches in search of buried treasure. Buried City might have sand, but it’s fair to say the rest of the maps are far enough from the shore to be useless in this endeavour.

What Changes Will The Beachcombing Map Condition Make In ARC Raiders?

The main change made during Beachcombing is that you can uncover items along the shore by using a Dockmaster’s Detector.

Considering that it’s only a Minor Map Condition, it makes sense that nothing drastic has changed, but it does pose a nice chance to earn some extra loot. With a Dockmaster’s Detector in hand, you can scan the shoreline for hidden loot.

Be careful, though, as you can uncover some hidden dangers long buried under the sands. Don’t expect to find anything groundbreaking in your searches, but if you’re after some extra cash, this is a great way to do it.

Here’s the full list of changes made to Riven Tides during the Beachcombing Minor Map Condition:

  • Buried Valuables
  • Lootable Detectors
  • Increased amount of Birds

Where To Find Dockmaster’s Detector In ARC Raiders?

Generally speaking, the Dockmaster’s Detector can only be found during the Beachcombing Map Condition.

However, to get your first one, all you need to do is complete the first stage of the Avian Alarm Project. Once you set up the bird cages by the buoys, you’ll be rewarded with your first Dockmaster’s Detector.

  • More Insider Gaming: Last Resort Event In ARC Raiders—All Rewards & How To Earn Merits, Explained

With this in hand, you can scan the beach on Riven Tides to uncover buried treasure, with some valuable trinkets up for grabs. Just roam the beach, and occasionally scan with the Detector. If it goes orange, you’re nearby, so check around that area until it goes green. Eventually, you’ll find a spot to dig, where you can find buried suitcases—and even weapon cases!

However, if you die with it on you or lose it for some reason, you can find more scattered across the map. As things stand, there’s no certain place that they can spawn, but you have to search during the Beachcombing Map Condition. It’s likely they’ll spawn in valuable containers, but it seems largely down to RNG.

Will you be playing the Beachcombing Minor Map Condition in ARC Raiders? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more ARC Raiders content, check out the following guides:

  • How To Get Rare Canto SMG In ARC Raiders—Blueprint Location, Explained
  • How To Get Legendary Dolabra Shotgun In ARC Raiders—Blueprint Location, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Valve Reveals Why New Steam Controller is Launching Before Steam Machine
item.content:encoded:
The Steam Controller is arriving before the Steam Machine because it doesn’t require RAM, as Valve is already dealing with memory shortages.

Valve’s new Steam Controller is something we have been hearing about a lot, and now we finally have a launch date: the device releases on May 4. The makers of the Steam Machine also recently addressed the device’s development status, stating that the release is close. They have now explained why one is being released before the other.

Steam Controller Launches Early Since It Doesn’t Require RAM

In a new interview with Polygon, Valve hardware engineer Steve Cardinali revealed why the Steam Controller launched earlier. Cardinali stated that, as it doesn’t have “RAM in it”, it is not impacted by memory shortage issues, and bringing the product to the consumers will not be a complicated process. The hardware engineer added that they are ready and working to have the quantity ready for launch; however, demand may exceed expectations.

Steam Machine is taking the brunt of the memory shortage, with Valve revealing that it is reassessing the shipping schedule and pricing for the Steam Machine as well as the Steam Frame VR Headset. Later on, Valve released another update, stating that they “hope to ship in 2026”, but memory shortages were presenting a serious challenge, and they would share their plans publicly when finalized. We have yet to receive a release window for the Steam Machine.

In other news, Valve is not the only one suffering from memory issues, as Xbox has also revealed that Project Helix’s pricing and availability will be affected by RAM shortages. Additionally, a dataminer has found mentions of SteamGPT, which could help with platform support and CS2 anti-cheat. What are your thoughts on Valve releasing the Steam Machine early due to memory shortages? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the first update on Resident Evil Requiem’s story DLC. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem Devs Give First Update On Story DLC
item.content:encoded:
Millions of players have likely already finished Resident Evil Requiem. You’ve endured the peril and pain of escorting Grace, and you’ve spinkicked your fair share of Zombies in the face with Leon, but story DLC is a necessity to learn more about RE9.

This is not the biggest update in the world, unfortunately, as it seems that Capcom is keeping quite hush-hush on the Resident Evil Requiem story DLC for now, but it’s the first time the team has publicly spoken about upcoming content, outside of the original social media announcement.

Resident Evil Requiem Story DLC Still In Development

On the plus side, development is still very much underway for the Resident Evil Requiem story DLC.

We learned a lot from a translated interview with Denfaminicogamer, featuring a conversation with Akishi Nakanishi (RE9 director) and Masato Kumazawa (RE9 producer).

There was the big reveal that Requiem’s mini-game arrival is imminent.

Sadly, Nakanishi couldn’t offer much about the in-development content, but did say:

“Regarding the additional story DLC, at this stage, I can only say that it is in production…I hope you look forward to it.”

If you’ve been waiting for news on the DLC, we did learn a while ago from the ever-reliable Dusk Golem that the timeline for the post-launch story DLC has been interrupted slightly:

“In an alternate timeline, RE9 would’ve come out in 2025, & the DLC for it would’ve come out in 2026 to bridge the gap between RE9 & Code Veronica Remake/Zero Remake, similar to Shadows of Rose RE8 DLC for RE8. It’s just due to RE9 getting delayed they’ll be close together.”

So, we could be looking at late 2026 for the DLC, or it could be pushed back into 2027. This wouldn’t be a shock as Capcom is working on multiple projects right now, including a strongly rumored Resident Evil: Code Veronica Remake.

The possibilities for Resident Evil Requiem story DLC are endless: What has Leon been up to? Where is Claire? Has Ada been up to no good? More specifically, what do YOU want to see from Resident Evil 9’s story DLC? Share your suggestions via our free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about the ARC Raiders Riven Tide Update 1.26.0 Patch Notes, and the Call of Duty Movie director calling war games ‘Pathetic’ and that playing them is ‘Weak’.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…
